$meta.title: POLARIS Task 0.1 — adversarial truth corpus for tests/fixtures/polaris-workspace

conventions.paths: scope-relative, slash-normalized; never absolute; never SQLite row IDs

sections.truth: Hand-authored CORRECT answers, independent of current behavior. Later waves consume these as fixture-truth oracles. Where current v4 behavior disagrees, the disagreement is a live defect (see polaris-current-failures.test.js) or a scheduled capability (v5/v6).
sections.currentV4: VERIFIED current-behavior baseline (2026-07-15, real pipeline over the fixture workspace, hand-checked against sources). polaris-baseline.test.js asserts this ground with superset semantics on refs/edges: Wave 1 repairs may ADD rows (same-line dedup fix) but must never lose or alter what is recorded here. callee links recorded under edges[].resolved are the v4 COMPATIBILITY HEURISTIC (name-uniqueness), never binding proof.

resolutions.name: slAdd
resolutions.note: BOTH same-line occurrences are distinct facts. Current v4 collapses them to one row (defect G5).

resolutions.name: shFormat
resolutions.note: The call binds the PARAMETER declared on line 5, not the module function on line 1. Current v4 heuristically links the module def — plausible-but-wrong. v4 structural truth: the parameter is not even a persisted def row, so an honest v4 answer is candidates-without-proof, never a resolved module target.

resolutions.name: orphanHelper
resolutions.note: unimported.ts is a module (has exports); orphanHelper is declared in another module and never imported. Global name-uniqueness is not reachability. Current v4 heuristically resolves it.

resolutions.scenario: duplicate wrong-twin definitions behind an explicit import
resolutions.name: fetchTwinData
resolutions.note: Proof requires the import binding (line 1) plus twin-a export — v6 capability. Honest v4 answer: ambiguous with both twins as candidates (current v4 correctly leaves the edge null).

resolutions.name: Result
resolutions.note: The call resolves in the VALUE namespace to the function (line 5), never the interface (line 1). v4 has no namespace split: honest answer is ambiguous (current v4 correctly leaves it null).

resolutions.name: aliasedHelper
resolutions.note: Current v4 leaves this null (no def named aliasedHelper) — honest, but blind to the alias binding it already persists.

resolutions.name: targetNs.importHelper
resolutions.note: Requires the namespace import proof chain (v6).

resolutions.name: ghostFn
resolutions.note: Import source './not-yet-created' does not exist. When a test creates it, the answer must transition to resolved WITH proof — the negative dependency must invalidate.

resolutions.name: originFn
resolutions.note: First occurrence proves through direct.ts (named re-export); second through star.ts (star re-export, column 31 — currently LOST to same-line dedup, defect G5).

resolutions.scenario: star re-export is not an import (cyclic pair)
resolutions.name: cycAlpha
resolutions.note: cycle-b.ts star-EXPORTS cycle-a but never imports it; cycAlpha is not in scope. Current v4 heuristically resolves it (globally unique) — plausible-but-wrong.

resolutions.scenario: python: module function shadowed by same-name method (lexical, not class scope)
resolutions.name: py_greet
resolutions.note: Python class bodies are not in the lexical chain of methods: the bare call binds the MODULE function (line 5), never the method (line 14). Current v4: null (two same-file candidates).

resolutions.scenario: go: stdlib namespace members are external
resolutions.name: fmt.Println

resolutions.scenario: rust: receiver method call needs type information
resolutions.name: insert

resolutions.scenario: java: same-class method call, same-line repeat
resolutions.name: javaHelper

resolutions.scenario: java: static import of a JDK member is external
resolutions.name: max

exports.note: Explicit export facts — schema v4 persists NONE of these (verified: direct.ts and star.ts index with zero rows). v5 ast_exports must cover every entry.

structuralOnly.note: Indexed with structural facts but no semantic profile: resolution/member questions answer structural or unsupported, never invented bindings.
